commit.message:
Initial OpenTelemetry Dashboards (#27)

* split serving and eventing service monitors

* adjust test scripts

* move promstack values to a new file

* drop README.md the settings it recommends is the default

* include initial serving dashboards

* include vendor'ed libraries

* update owners to allow eventing & serving writers to approve changes

* simplify directories structure

* include port-forward script

* adjust test scripts to work

* use NIGHTLY_BUILD env var to be more explicit

* adjust dashboards folder

* introduce basic eventing dashboard

* move promstack-values.yaml to the root directory

* promote knative attributes

* install eventing dashboard in test script

issues.title: README update
issues.body:
Hi,

The README has a link to a grafana folder that does not lead anywhere:
> Once kube-state-metrics has been adjusted, you will need to deploy the following servicemonitors and import the dashboards in the [grafana](https://github.com/knative-extensions/monitoring/blob/main/grafana) folder.


also, the links still points to old name "knative-sandbox":
> kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/knative-sandbox/monitoring/main/config/eventing-monitors.yaml

issues.title: When there are no requests it prints no namespace for the template variable:
issues.body:
When there are no requests it prints no namespace for the template variable:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7945591/123631051-fb336500-d81e-11eb-8b9a-13286768962f.png)
However I had a service running without any requests hitting it. Namespace could cover this case too if i was based on (not a big issue), probably we should list the service and show no data as before:
```
"query": "label_values(kube_pod_labels{label_serving_knative_dev_configuration=~\".+\"}, namespace)",
          
```

_Originally posted by @skonto in https://github.com/knative-sandbox/monitoring/pull/1#discussion_r659713049_

pullRequests.title: Initial OpenTelemetry Dashboards
pullRequests.body:
This introduces the initial OpenTelemetry dashboards. Probably important to note that these are 'best-effort'

The dashboards are now defined in 'code' using jsonnet + a grafana jsonnet lib (https://github.com/grafana/grafonnet)

This makes it easy to edit the dashboard and generate the configmaps. Prior it would be manually edited and exported.

/assign @Cali0707 @evankanderson 

Note: I'm deleting the older dashboards as they exist in a separate 'opencensus' branch (https://github.com/knative-extensions/monitoring/tree/opencensus). This was necessary not to break older documentation.
